channel.title: NPR Topics: World
channel.description: NPR world news, international art and culture, world business and financial markets, world economy, and global trends in health, science and technology. Subscribe to the World Story of the Day podcast and RSS feed.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Afghanistan says 400 people killed in Pakistan strike on Kabul hospital
item.description: Afghanistan has accused Pakistan of targeting a hospital for drug users in the Afghan capital with an airstrike, marking a dramatic escalation of a conflict that began late last month. Pakistan has dismissed the accusation.
item.content:encoded:
Afghanistan has accused Pakistan of targeting a hospital for drug users in the Afghan capital with an airstrike, marking a dramatic escalation of a conflict that began late last month. Pakistan has dismissed the accusation.

(Image credit: Barackatullah Popal)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Supreme Court to hear expedited arguments on protected status for migrants
item.description: The court temporarily blocked the Trump administration from deporting some 6,000 Syrians and 350,000 Haitians who were granted Temporary Protected Status.
item.content:encoded:
The court temporarily blocked the Trump administration from deporting some 6,000 Syrians and 350,000 Haitians who were granted Temporary Protected Status.

(Image credit: Kevin Dietsch)
item.dc:creator: Zoe Sobel

item.title: What are Trump's options in Iran as oil stops flowing and allies resist joining war?
item.description: Iran has effectively shut down the flow of oil from the Gulf region. President Trump hasn't offered a clear solution. Trump is calling for allies to assist the U.S. military in removing the Iranian threat, and many of the allies are resisting.
item.content:encoded: Iran has effectively shut down the flow of oil from the Gulf region. President Trump hasn't offered a clear solution. Trump is calling for allies to assist the U.S. military in removing the Iranian threat, and many of the allies are resisting.
item.dc:creator: Mary Louise Kelly

item.title: EU rejects Trump's request to help secure the Strait of Hormuz
item.description: The European Union has rejected President Trump's request to help secure the Strait of Hormuz, saying the war is "not theirs."
item.content:encoded: The European Union has rejected President Trump's request to help secure the Strait of Hormuz, saying the war is "not theirs."
item.dc:creator: Teri Schultz

item.title: Cuba hit by island-wide blackout as energy crisis deepens
item.description: On Monday Cuba was plunged into an island-wide blackout affecting 11 million people after a "complete disconnection" of its electrical system, officials said, amid a worsening fuel shortage.
item.content:encoded:
On Monday Cuba was plunged into an island-wide blackout affecting 11 million people after a "complete disconnection" of its electrical system, officials said, amid a worsening fuel shortage.

(Image credit: Ramon Espinosa)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Senate prepares to vote on Trump's SAVE Act. And, takeaways from last night's Oscars
item.description: Senate Republicans are gearing up to vote on President Trump's controversial voting overhaul, the SAVE America Act. And, key takeaways from the 2026 Oscars.
item.content:encoded:
Senate Republicans are gearing up to vote on President Trump's controversial voting overhaul, the SAVE America Act. And, key takeaways from the 2026 Oscars.

(Image credit: Chip Somodevilla)
item.dc:creator: Brittney Melton

item.title: Washington Post's David Ignatius says war on Iran won't change the country's regime
item.description: Michel Martin speaks to Washington Post columnist David Ignatius, who writes that the U.S. war on Iran, despite any tactical success, leaves the regime in place for the foreseeable future.
item.content:encoded: Michel Martin speaks to Washington Post columnist David Ignatius, who writes that the U.S. war on Iran, despite any tactical success, leaves the regime in place for the foreseeable future.
item.dc:creator: Michel Martin

item.title: Trump demands NATO and China police the Strait of Hormuz. So far they aren't joining
item.description: With the Iran war entering a third week, Israel said it plans for at least three more weeks of war, while President Trump demanded other countries help the U.S. secure the vital Strait of Hormuz.
item.content:encoded:
With the Iran war entering a third week, Israel said it plans for at least three more weeks of war, while President Trump demanded other countries help the U.S. secure the vital Strait of Hormuz.

(Image credit: Altaf Qadri)
item.dc:creator: NPR Staff

item.title: Hungary's longtime prime minister rallies to keep seat as rival calls for a new era
item.description: Hungarian Prime Minister Viktor Orban slams Europe in an election speech on his country's Revolution Day.
item.content:encoded: Hungarian Prime Minister Viktor Orban slams Europe in an election speech on his country's Revolution Day.
item.dc:creator: Rob Schmitz

item.title: Iran blocks vital oil route as Israel expands its ground operations in Lebanon
item.description: As the war in Iran enters its third week, there's no end in sight. Iran has blocked the Strait of Hormuz, a vital oil route, as Trump urges allies to act and Israel expands its ground operations.
item.content:encoded: As the war in Iran enters its third week, there's no end in sight. Iran has blocked the Strait of Hormuz, a vital oil route, as Trump urges allies to act and Israel expands its ground operations.
item.dc:creator: Daniel Estrin
